semantic.role: Pipeline stage task for semantic architecture validation of source files against project rules.
responsibilities: Read the current project index to identify source files requiring validation.
For each source file, retrieve applicable rules from the rule engine and evaluate compliance.
Normalize any raw violation data into the canonical RuleViolation format using normalizeRuleViolations.
Record validated violations into the project manifest via taskCtx.manifest.recordViolation and push diagnostic summaries via pushValidateDiagnostics.
Build prompt artifacts and relevance diagnostics to enrich validation context for downstream stages.
Produce a ValidationTaskResult containing violation diagnostics, a summary, and execution metadata for use by subsequent pipeline stages.
outOfScope: Parsing CLI arguments or defining new CLI commands.
Managing repository initialization, configuration, or credentials.
Executing MCP protocol operations or serving tool requests.
Performing source code scanning or AST extraction (handled by scanner stage).
Orchestrating unrelated pipeline stages such as aggregation, fix, or synchronization.
invariants: The module must implement stage-local validation work on top of core contracts and engines, and must not take over CLI command parsing or unrelated service orchestration (Rule: task-stage-boundary).
All rule violations must be recorded through the manifest and not stored independently.
The task must use the common task context and checkpoint mechanisms to allow correct pipeline execution and resumption.

changeIntent.architecturalIntent: Implement a reusable validation stage that checks source files against architecture rules and produces normalized diagnostics for the pipeline.
changeIntent.recentChangeIntent: Apply release gate fixes and sync accumulated source changes, likely improving task stage boundary compliance and integrating with the prompt-context/prompt-policy refactor.

publicSurface.description: Normalizes an unknown input value into a stable array of RuleViolation objects by filtering and sanitizing entries. Used by validation consumers to ensure consistent violation data regardless of the source format.

Main pipeline task class that executes the semantic architecture audit stage. Extends SpineTask, iterates over indexed files, evaluates rules, records violations, and outputs a ValidationTaskResult for downstream stages.

Interface that encapsulates high-level summary statistics of the validation run, such as total violations, counts per severity, and files processed. Consumed by pipeline orchestrators and reporting views.

Interface defining the full output contract of the validation task, including violation diagnostics, a summary, and execution metadata. Designed to be consumed by subsequent pipeline stages and view services.
